scenario: By 2030, platforms will review 100 billion interactions daily. What percentage will be moderated by AI?
insight: AI will scale moderation — humans will define the rules. Volume will be too massive for human review. AI will handle detection, triage, and enforcement for the vast majority of cases, while humans focus on edge cases, appeals, and cultural nuance.

scenario: Online identity will evolve dramatically. Which future is most likely by 2030?
insight: Identity will become a trust signal — not a single requirement. The future internet will support verified real identities, pseudonymous accounts, and AI agents acting on behalf of users. Trust systems will depend on layered identity and reputation signals.

scenario: Bad actors will increasingly deploy AI-powered attacks. What becomes the dominant defense?
insight: The future of T&S will be an AI vs AI arms race. Bad actors are already using generative AI for scams, deepfakes, and automation. Defense will increasingly rely on AI models that can detect adversarial patterns faster than humans can.

scenario: Governments are rapidly expanding platform accountability. Which regulatory future is most likely?
insight: Trust & Safety will become as much regulatory infrastructure as operational. We already see fragmentation: EU Digital Services regulations, US sector-based rules, and APAC national frameworks — platforms will operate under regional compliance regimes, not a single global standard.

scenario: In 2030, the largest Trust & Safety threat will be:
insight: The next wave of platform abuse will be financially motivated. Fraud scales fastest with AI — voice cloning, deepfake impersonation, automated phishing networks, and social engineering bots all make AI-powered fraud the most attractive vector for attackers.

scenario: Which area will see the largest Trust & Safety investment by 2030?
insight: AI safety will become core Trust & Safety infrastructure. With generative AI creating content at scale, platforms will invest heavily in safe AI deployment, model guardrails, and misuse detection — making AI safety the defining investment of the decade.

scenario: Which Trust & Safety metric will matter most by 2030?
insight: The best harm is the harm that never happens. The industry will move from reactive moderation to proactive prevention — platforms that measure harm prevented, not just harm actioned, will set the benchmark for the field.

scenario: Which product design shift will define safer platforms by 2030?
insight: Safety will increasingly be designed into the product. The most effective safety models will be built directly into product architecture — not layered on as moderation after the fact — making safer defaults the foundational design principle.

scenario: By 2030, human moderators will handle the most complex and harmful edge cases. What will define the future of workforce wellbeing?
insight: AI will become the frontline shield for human moderator wellbeing. The biggest shift will be AI acting as a protective filter so humans only review the most necessary edge cases — dramatically reducing exposure to traumatic content and redefining the moderator role.
